message:
feat(codegen): bounded equality-prefix SeekGE scan for composite index ORDER BY elimination

Rewrite the VDBE ORDER BY index-scan codegen to support composite
indexes where the leading columns are constrained by equality in
the WHERE clause and the ORDER BY matches the subsequent columns.
Previously, index-ordered scans always used Rewind/Last to scan
the entire index; now they can SeekGE directly into the matching
prefix slice and break on the first non-matching key.

codegen.rs — codegen_select_index_ordered_scan:
- Build a probe key record from the equality prefix expressions
  with trailing NULLs and i64::MIN rowid, then SeekGE to the
  first matching entry
- Emit per-key-position Column + Ne guards after each Next to
  break when the cursor moves past the equality prefix boundary
- Use index_key_collation_p4 and index_key_cmp_p5 for correct
  collation and affinity on prefix comparisons
- Save and restore anon placeholder base so WHERE filter
  parameter numbering stays correct across the bounded scan
- Fall back to the original Rewind/Last path when no equality
  prefix is available or scan is descending

codegen.rs — resolve_order_by_index_plan:
- Refactored from single-index matching to best-plan search
  across all table indexes
- OrderByIndexPlan now stores the full IndexSchema (not just name
  + root_page) and an equality_prefix_len field
- Score candidates by equality_prefix_len first, then by covering
  output availability, selecting the best index
- Skip descending scans with equality prefix (not yet supported)
- Skip indexes whose key terms are individually DESC in the
  equality prefix range

codegen.rs — new helper functions:
- collect_conjunctive_terms: flatten AND trees into a Vec of terms
- extract_index_column_equality_expr: match col = const terms
  against a specific index column
- extract_index_equality_prefix_exprs: extract the longest
  equality prefix from a WHERE clause for a given index
- index_key_cmp_p5: compute column affinity flag for key comparisons
- index_key_collation_p4: extract per-key-position collation

connection.rs — test expansion:
- Expand test_order_by_equality_prefix_on_composite_index to also
  verify the manual codegen path (ProgramBuilder + codegen_select)
  emits SeekGE and avoids SorterOpen, in addition to the existing
  explain_opcodes check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
